title: tillaea erecta subsp. eremica
intro: Tillaea erecta subsp. eremica, also known as the Sandwort plant, is a type of succulent native to Western Australia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tillaea erecta subsp. eremica include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce skin irritations, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the leaves in a salve or ointment to apply topically.
